title: hieracium pallescens subsp. gelmianum
intro: Hieracium pallescens subsp. gelmianum, commonly known as a subspecies of yellow hawkweed, is a flowering plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. It belongs to the dandelion-like plants and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat wounds and skin issues due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's health benefits include its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its use as a diuretic to treat urinary tract issues. In herbal preparations, Hieracium pallescens subsp. gelmianum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be applied topically to skin conditions or consumed orally to support overall health.
